questions.question: In a @OneToMany relationship, which entity owns the relationship (has the foreign key column in its table)?
options: The 'one' side — the User table has an orders_id column
The 'many' side — the Order table has a user_id column
Both tables have a foreign key pointing to each other
A separate join table always manages the foreign key

questions.question: What does FetchType.LAZY mean for a @OneToMany collection?
options: The collection is loaded asynchronously in a background thread
The collection is loaded slower due to extra SQL overhead
The collection is not loaded until you call getOrders() — a separate SQL query runs at that point
The collection is never loaded — you must use a custom query

questions.question: What is the N+1 SELECT problem?
options: A bug where Spring executes the same query N+1 times due to a caching error
For N parent entities, N additional queries are fired to load each parent's lazy collection — O(N) database round-trips instead of 1
A database constraint violation when inserting N+1 rows exceeds the table limit
A pagination bug where page N+1 returns the same data as page N

questions.question: What SQL does @EntityGraph(attributePaths = {'orders'}) generate compared to a plain findById?
options: It generates the same query but caches the orders result
It generates SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ? plus a separate SELECT for orders
It generates a single LEFT JOIN query: SELECT u.*, o.* FROM users u LEFT JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id WHERE u.id = ?
It generates a stored procedure call on the database

questions.question: For @ManyToOne, what is the DEFAULT fetch type and what should you change it to?
options: Default: LAZY — should change to EAGER for better performance
Default: EAGER — should change to LAZY to avoid loading the parent entity unnecessarily
Default: LAZY — no change needed, LAZY is always correct
Default: EAGER — EAGER is correct for @ManyToOne and should be kept
